content_preview.title: Crimson Desert Sells Three Million Copies in Five Days but Undisclosed AI Art and Mixed Reviews Send Pearl Abyss Stock Down 30 Percent
content_preview.summary: Crimson Desert sold three million copies in five days, but Pearl Abyss's stock fell 30 percent after mixed reviews, and undisclosed AI-generated artwork forced an apology and Steam policy violation disclosure.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Crimson Desert, the open-world action RPG from South Korean studio Pearl Abyss, sold three million copies within five days of its March 19 launch, establishing it as one of the fastest-selling new intellectual properties in recent gaming history. Yet the commercial success has been overshadowed by a [nearly 30 percent stock collapse](https://www.gamespot.com/articles/crimson-desert-devs-stock-plunges-following-the-games-reviews/1100-6538890/) driven by mixed critical reviews, and a separate cont...

recommendations: 1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Compelling gaming industry narrative combining commercial success, stock crash, and AI art controversy.
editor_notes.source_verification: Engadget (fetched): Confirms Pearl Abyss apology, AI-generated 2D props, Steam disclosure, comprehensive audit commitment. GameSpot (2x 403): Reputable but blocked — stock drop and AI art details. Kotaku (403): Blocked — sales/budget data. PC Gamer: Review context.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: AI art controversy fully verified via Engadget. Financial data (3M copies, 30% stock drop, $133.5M budget) from blocked but reputable sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. Good tension between commercial success and controversy.
concerns: 3 of 5 sources returned 403. Engadget confirms the AI art angle. Financial claims rely on blocked GameSpot/Kotaku sources, but these are reputable gaming outlets.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Engaging gaming news with partial verification. Approved given source reputation.
